title: Phlomoides korovinii
intro: Phlomoides korovinii, also known as Korovin's phlomoides, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wound healing, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Phlomoides korovinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phlomoides korovinii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phlomoides korovinii is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Phlomoides, which is a group of flowering plants native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Within the genus Phlomoides, P. korovinii is further classified as a species of the Lamiaceae family. The exact classification of P. korovinii is Phlomoides korovinii (Rydb.) Gontsch., which includes its genus and species name along with the original describer's name in parentheses. This classification helps in the identification and categorization of the plant within the botanical community.
